May Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala accept it and make it blessed. A blessed transfer of knowledge and inheritance from our prophet sallall.aii wasallam to our hearts. We usually begin with a small reminder, something to kind of get the juices flowing. And Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala tells us he tells us to remind one another to remind why because the reminder is of benefit and consequence for the believers. Many of us we have a lot more knowledge than we probably give ourselves credit for in the sense that throughout our years most of us especially born as Muslims grew up in a Muslim household we've heard a lot of commonplace narrations verses a lot of things that are very well known and common place in our religion. However, years and years go by and the sharpness of these things starts to dull in our minds. This is why it is so important to constantly read the Quran over and over and over. Never a person's journey with the Quran never stops over and over and over. Every time they finish it, they turn it over and read it again. As a matter of fact, some scholars would advise that a person never leave on the exact end of the Quran. They would restart it before they end their final sitting of the evening or the day with it. Why? So that it is in a constant loop. And the prophet sallallah.aii alaihi wasallam commanded that we recite the Quran at least once a month. At least once a month. And alkari and Muslim the prophet sallallah.aihi Wasam. It was brought to his attention that one of his most devout companions and the scholars of the companions. He had just gotten married. But when his father went to visit him, he found that his wife was muted. and she wasn't dressed nice. She didn't look she was you know was taking care of herself. And usually this articulates a problem in the household. Why? Because naturally a woman will want to you know grab the attention or catch the eye of her husband and vice versa. And so he asked her what the issue is and she told him that Abdullah is one of the best men as a slave to Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala. But he has no use for marriage or women. The guy is constantly fasting. He was praying all night every night. So he'd never sleep next to her. He is fasting every single day and he is reading the Quran every day once a day the whole Quran. And so the prophet sallallah.aii wasallam commanded him to lessen of that and the most he allowed him to fast every day is how much? Anyone know the narration? What is the maximum allowance for fasting for a person? How much? Very good. Which is fasting one day, breaking your fast the other. So every other day and the most a person can pray in the night is the same narration. The prophet sallallahu alaihi wasallam mentioned the of of and that a person should sleep a part of the night and pray a part of the night. And in commanding him to recite the Quran, he said read it once a month, once every 30 days. In some wordings of this narration, once every 40 days. He said, He said, "I can do better than that. I can do more than that." He said then once every 10 days and he continued to go down 14 days, 10 days, 7 days, once a week until he said sai wasam that the most is that once every 3 days and anything faster than once every 3 days, a person is generally unable to unable to be consistent with that and second of all unable to contemplate the Quran properly because now they're in too much of a hurry trying to finish its recitation. So some of the scholars of Islam derived from this narration and took from this narration that a Muslim who is able to read the Quran has no excuse to not read it at least once a month once every 30 days should complete the mus the Quran. And if a person doesn't have the ability to do so then they should learn. There's nothing stopping a person today, whether they're blind or even if they're deaf, from learning the recitation of the Quran. We've got entire mah in braille. Now, literacy is at an all-time high worldwide. There's no reason a person cannot learn to recite the book of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala and give it of his time. However, person should not be extreme in anything that he or she does and part of that is the recitation of the Quran. Person does not push himself until he burns out nor does he ignore the Quran until he forgets it. Now, so having said that, inshallah we will start with any questions. summary: This is a weekly question and answer session from the Muslim Community Center of Tucson featuring Islamic teachings on Quranic recitation, prophecy, and interpersonal rights. The speaker begins with a reminder about the importance of regular Quranic recitation, citing the Prophet's guidance that Muslims should complete the Quran at least once monthly, with the ideal frequency being once every three days. The discussion then addresses how to reconcile prophecies about the end times with the Islamic obligation to strive and improve society, explaining that prophecies are not necessarily prohibitions or commands. The session concludes with a detailed discussion on the concept of oppression and rights between Muslims on the Day of Judgment, distinguishing between quantifiable physical rights and matters of personal expectations.
suggested_title: Weekly Q&A Session: Quranic Recitation, Divine Decree and Rights Between Muslims
catchy_title: How Often Should You Read the Quran?
